LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AMERITEX APPAREL CORPORATION MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT CONTINUED FROM JUN-12-2003 TO LAW AND MOTION AT JUL-10-2003, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. APPEARANCES: FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, APPEARED THROUGH COURTCALL AT 11:25 AM AFTER THE COURT HEARD THE MOTION WITH THE PLAINTIFF, IN PROPRIA PERSONA. AFTER A SHORT ARGUMENT, THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER OFF CALENDAR BASED UPON THE REPRESENTATION OF PLAINTIFF FOR NOT HAVING PROPER NOTICE. AT 11:25 AM, DUE TO THE UNEXPECTED CIRCUMSTANCE WITH COURTCALL, DEFENDANT WAS UNABLE TO ARGUE MOTION. IN VIEW OF THIS, THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER UNTIL JULY 10, 2003 AT 9:30 AM. MOVING PARTY TO GIVE NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF. HON. PAUL H. ALVARADO. REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR #5220

No opposition was filed, and the motion to clarify is granted by written order vacating the July 29, 2003 prove-up judgement against Defendant Ameritex Apparel Corporation, ordering Plaintiffs to return property delivered to them by Defendant in accordance with said judgement, and granting Defendant Ameritex 10 days to file an answer or an amended answer. Default Prove Up In Scd 218 At 09:00 Am On April 28, 2003
Off Calendar Jul-30-2003 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove Up
Judge Ollie Marie-Victoire, CSR: Brenda Fields,Clerks: S. Ravinantapricha/M. Vallejo
Plaintiffs: Daniel Cunneen and Storm Earl Jenkins were sworn & examined.
Jeffrey L. Malek, Esq. for defendant Asia Apparel, LLC.
Defendant's attorney entered a motion to continue hearing to September 2, 2003- the motion was-Denied.
Defendant's attorney informed the court that a Motion to Set Aside Entry of Default is under submission in L&M Dept. 302. He also Objected to granting the Prove Up without waiting for the ruling on the motion to set aside and without the court having the case file.
After hearing the evidence presented, plaintiff's declarations, the Court rules in favor of the plaintiffs Daniel Cunneen and Storm Earl Jenkins against the defendant Asia Apparel, LLC
as follows:
Defendant to return all materials and photographs to Plaintiff.
Plaintiff to submit a written judgment.
